codeReview.summary: Clean, focused implementation in a single file (CreativeSparkDesktopLayout.tsx). Replaces the always-visible right sidebar with a collapsible conversation panel (hidden by default, framer-motion spring slide-in from right) and persistent fixed-bottom controls. All expected behaviors are met: panel starts hidden with a toggle button, AnimatePresence handles open/close animations, controls use fixed positioning so they never scroll, gallery remains full-width and interactive when panel overlays from the right. No AgentVisualizer, tool toggles, or model selector are imported or rendered. Good accessibility (aria-label), testability (data-testid attributes), and design consistency (dark theme, orange accents, glassy blur effects).

issues.description: Toggle button position when panel is open uses hardcoded 'right-[416px]' which is PANEL_WIDTH(400) + 16px. If PANEL_WIDTH changes, this Tailwind class won't automatically update. Consider using a CSS calc or deriving from the constant.
Gallery bottom padding 'pb-[140px]' is a magic number tied to the persistent controls height. If controls height changes, this padding won't auto-adjust. A minor maintainability concern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The PANEL_WIDTH constant pattern (module-level const for layout dimensions) is used here but not documented in frontend-knowledge.md. This is a minor pattern that other Creative Spark features might want to follow for consistent panel sizing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: CreativeSparkDesktopLayout.tsx:13 — const PANEL_WIDTH = 400; used in animation offsets, inline style, and toggle button positioning.

summary: Pass. The implementation correctly fulfills all 5 validation criteria (VAL-GALLERY-006 through VAL-GALLERY-010). The conversation panel is hidden by default with a visible toggle, slides in with framer-motion spring animation, persistent controls are fixed at the bottom, and no Open Studio elements (AgentVisualizer, tool toggles, model selector) are rendered. Two non-blocking issues noted: a hardcoded Tailwind class that shadows the PANEL_WIDTH constant, and a magic number for bottom padding. Code quality is high — good component decomposition, accessibility, and adherence to the brainstorm design system.
